data.title: As a sprint orchestrator, I can detect drift when a related epic closes after implementation planning
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Extend sprint-drift-check.sh to follow relates_to links on the current epic. When a related epic transitioned to closed after the implementation plan was created, emit REPLAN_TRIGGER with the closed epic ID and a summary of what changed.
**Why**: Related epics that close may change assumptions or introduce capabilities that affect in-progress implementation plans. Without drift detection across relates_to links, coordinating epics ship independently and miss coordination opportunities.
**Scope**:
- IN: Query relates_to neighbors via ticket CLI, compare closure timestamps against implementation plan timestamp, emit REPLAN_TRIGGER per existing contract, one-hop only (direct neighbors), guard clause for empty relates_to set, error handling for ticket CLI failures, fallback to story creation timestamp when implementation plan timestamp is missing
- OUT: Recursive neighbor traversal (neighbors-of-neighbors), new scripts (extends existing sprint-drift-check.sh), coupling-strength filtering, brainstorm suggestion logic

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, sprint-drift-check.sh queries relates_to links for the current epic and emits REPLAN_TRIGGER when a related epic closed after the implementation plan was created, using the same contract format as existing git-history drift detection
  ← Satisfies: "sprint-drift-check.sh follows relates_to links on the current epic: when a related epic transitioned to closed after the implementation plan was created, emit REPLAN_TRIGGER with the closed epic ID and a summary of what changed — using the same contract as git-history drift detection"
- When this story is complete, drift detection checks only direct relates_to neighbors and does not traverse neighbors-of-neighbors
  ← Satisfies: "Drift detection is one-hop only: checks direct relates_to neighbors, not neighbors-of-neighbors"
- When this story is complete, an epic with no relates_to links exits drift detection cleanly without errors
  ← Satisfies: "Empty relates_to set must exit cleanly" (scenario)
- When this story is complete, relates_to links where the related epic closed before the implementation plan was created do not trigger REPLAN_TRIGGER
  ← Satisfies: "Pre-existing closures must not trigger" (scenario)
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Ticket CLI failure during neighbor lookup must warn and continue, not abort drift detection
- [Reliability] Missing implementation plan timestamp should fall back to story creation timestamp
- [Testing] Existing sprint-drift-check.sh test suite should be extended, not replaced
